QUESTION:
Are any laboratory parameters trending outside normal limits in specific patient populations?

Answer the question based on the log entries above. Be specific and cite entry IDs in [brackets] when referencing data points.

message.content:
**Short answer:**  
Across all of the daily‑operations logs the laboratory values that are reported for the two major sub‑populations (statin‑concomitant vs. non‑statin, and the three treatment arms – multimodal, standard, placebo) remain within the usual clinical reference ranges. No single parameter is consistently crossing a “high‑” or “low‑” threshold for any group. The only measurable shift is a modest rise in the inflammatory marker **ESR**, which climbs from the high‑teens/low‑twenties early in the study to the mid‑20s in the later weeks, but even those values are still below the typical upper‑limit of ≈ 30 mm/hr and therefore not an out‑of‑range abnormality.

---

### Detailed evidence by entry

| Population / Parameter | Normal reference range
message.reasoning:
We need to assess if any lab parameters are trending outside normal limits in specific patient populations. We have multiple entries: ep (statin vs non-statin) and dx (treatment arms). Need to look for trends where values exceed normal ranges. Normal ranges typical: ALT ~7-56 U/L, AST 10-40, Bilirubin ~0.1-1.2, ALP 44-147, Creatinine ~0.6-1.3, BUN 7-20, WBC 4-11, Platelets 150-450, Hemoglobin 12-16, etc. Look at data across entries.

Statin subgroup ALT means: 25.5, 26.8, 26.0 – within normal. AST: 26.4, 22.1, 27.9 – within normal. Bilirubin: 0.78, 0.73, 0.59 – normal. ALP: 84.9, 71.0, 79.3 – normal. Creatinine: 1.06, 0.85, 1.04 – normal. BUN: 16.9, 16.6, 16.6 – normal. WBC: 5.9, 7.5, 5.9 – normal. Platelets: 233, 236, 245 – normal.

Non-Statin subgroup ALT: 29.9, 29.7, 21.2 – normal. AST: 26.4, 20.7, 29.1 – normal. Bilirubin: 0.73, 0.9, 0.54 – normal. ALP: 71.9, 56.7, 73.5 – normal. Creatinine: 1.1, 0.92, 1.0 – normal. BUN: 16.8, 16.0, 17.2 – normal. WBC: 6.0, 7.5, 5.6 – normal. Platelets: 218, 233, 229 – normal.

Now safety labs in dx entries: Hemoglobin around 13-14, normal. WBC around 6-8, normal. Platelets 221-247, normal. Serum Creatinine: values 1.05, 1.11, 0.94, 1.11, 1.0, 0.97, 1.01 – all within normal. Blood Glucose 87-101, normal. ESR varies 19,18,12,10,8,24,24 – ESR not a normal limit but can be elevated; values 19,18,12,10,8,24 – some higher early but trending down then up again. No clear out-of-range.

Any specific population trending? Perhaps placebo group shows higher VAS pain but labs? Not.

Look at any lab parameter trending upward across weeks: Creatinine seems slightly decreasing from 1.06 to 0.94 then back up to 1.11 then 1.0 then 0.97 then 1.01 – stable.

Platelets: 233, 218, 238, 232, 234, 237, 231, 221 – slight decline but within range.

WBC: 5.9,6.0,7.8,6.3,8.4,8.0,7.7,7.1 – slight increase early then stable.

ESR: 19,18,12,10,8,24,24 – increased at later weeks (week 6-7). Could be trending upward in later weeks across overall population.

Any specific patient population? Maybe multimodal arm shows higher creatinine? Check multimodal creatinine not given per arm.

Thus answer: No lab parameters outside normal limits; all within reference ranges. ESR shows slight upward trend but still within typical range (<30). So answer accordingly.
